questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language. It's the standard language for creating web pages.

questions.question: Which tag is used for the largest heading?
questions.explanation: <h1> is the largest heading. Headings range from <h1> (largest) to <h6> (smallest).

questions.question: Where does the <title> element go?
questions.explanation: The <title> element goes inside the <head> section. It defines the title shown in the browser tab.

questions.question: What does the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration do?
options: It defines a link to an external stylesheet
It tells the browser this is an HTML5 document
It creates a new HTML element
It adds a comment to the page
questions.explanation: <!DOCTYPE html> tells the browser that this document is an HTML5 document.

questions.question: Which element contains the visible content of a web page?
questions.explanation: The <body> element contains all the visible content that users see on the web page.

questions.question: Which input type creates a password field?
questions.explanation: type="password" creates an input that masks the characters as dots.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the <label> element?
options: To associate text with a form control for accessibility
questions.explanation: The <label> element associates descriptive text with a form control, improving accessibility.

questions.question: Which attribute specifies where to send form data?
questions.explanation: The 'action' attribute specifies the URL where form data should be sent when submitted.

questions.question: What element is used for multi-line text input?
questions.explanation: <textarea> creates a multi-line text input area, unlike <input> which is single-line.

questions.question: Which semantic element is best for the main navigation?
questions.explanation: <nav> is the semantic element designed specifically for navigation links.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of semantic HTML?
options: It improves accessibility, SEO, and code readability
questions.explanation: Semantic HTML improves accessibility (screen readers), SEO (search engines), and makes code more readable.

questions.question: Which element represents self-contained content?
questions.explanation: <article> represents self-contained content that could be independently distributed or reused.
